mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender 2.4 TDCi engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 2.4 TDCi engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with fitting adding £1,200–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Defender.

mainEntity.name: Is the Defender 2.4 TDCi (Puma) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the ZSD‑424 is a robust, chain-driven diesel with a cast-iron block, designed for heavy-duty use. However, it is sensitive to fuel quality and maintenance — injectors and EGR valves are the two most common failure points.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Defender 2.4 TDCi engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injector clogging or failure, EGR valve carbon build-up, and brake vacuum pump failure (pre‑2009 single‑seal design). The timing chain itself is durable and doesn't require scheduled replacement.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender 2.4 TDCi engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £8,000 Defender, a £4,500 recon is around 56% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon exceeds our 60% Land Rover threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender 2.4 TDCi engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 2.4 TDCi Puma engine code is ZSD‑424. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the injectors on a 2.4 TDCi, or do they need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injectors can often be reconditioned — cleaning and testing typically costs £400–£900 for a set. If they are beyond repair, replacement is the only option. At the first sign of rough running or ticking, book an inspection.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender 2.4 TDCi engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: Injector Failure (Clogging / Electrical)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, loss of power, increased fuel consumption, excessive exhaust smoke, engine rattle/ticking.
problems.repairCost: £400–£900 per set (reconditioned) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: The 2.4 TDCi's common-rail system is sensitive to fuel quality and filter condition. If you notice rough running or a ticking noise from the injectors, have them tested promptly. A set of reconditioned injectors is far cheaper than the engine damage that contaminated injectors can cause.

problems.title: EGR Valve Carbon Build-up
problems.symptoms: Reduced power, poor throttle response, increased emissions, occasional stalling at idle.
problems.repairCost: £200–£500 (cleaning or replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: The EGR valve on the Puma engine is prone to carbon clogging, especially if the vehicle is used for short journeys or low-speed off-roading. Periodic cleaning — or fitting an EGR blanking plate (where MOT-legal) — can prevent performance issues and keep the engine running smoothly.

problems.title: Brake Vacuum Pump Failure
problems.symptoms: Hard brake pedal, hissing noise from the pump area, loss of servo assistance.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: brake servo failure is a safety-critical issue

problems.recommendation: Early 2.4 Puma engines (pre-2009) had a single-seal vacuum pump design that is prone to failure. If you hear a hissing noise or the brake pedal feels unusually firm, have the pump inspected immediately — a failed pump can cause complete loss of brake assistance.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender 2.4 TDCi — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — before it causes piston or turbo damage
Preventative maintenance; improves performance and MPG
Viable on lower-mileage examples; assess overall engine condition

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Defender 2.4 TDCi? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented injector service history (or recent replacement)
* EGR valve cleaned or replaced within the last 30,000 miles
* Brake vacuum pump confirmed as the revised double-seal design (post-2009)
* Full service history with sub-10,000-mile oil changes
avoidIf: * Rough idle or ticking noise from the engine with no injector history
* No record of EGR maintenance and evidence of poor running
* Brake pedal feels hard or spongy — vacuum pump may be failing
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — common-rail system could be compromised

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 2.4 TDCi Puma is a fundamentally robust, chain-driven engine that replaced the TD5 with more refinement and Euro 4 compliance. But it's sensitive to fuel quality and maintenance — injectors and EGR are the two watchpoints. Buy on documented history, not just mileage, and factor in a preventative EGR clean if there's no record of one.

cta.label: → Compare Defender 2.4 TDCi engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender 2.4 TDCi (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for ticking or rattling; injector noise is the main concern on this era (no timing chain rattle — it's a chain, not a belt)
* Service History — including engine era (ZSD-424 Puma) and confirmed oil-change discipline
* Smoke Check — Excessive black or white smoke can indicate injector or EGR issues
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or power loss?
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear (Euro 4 DPF-equipped models)
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
